text.title: trade-off-stakeholder-communication Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for trade-off-stakeholder-communication as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stakeholder surprise: trade-offs discovered in production by stakeholders who were not informed during the decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Late discovery of trade-offs creates distrust and triggers retroactive debates about a decision that is already implemented. Early stakeholder involvement at the decision point is cheaper than production incident management.

failure-mode.description: Hidden trade-offs: not documenting what we sacrifice with the chosen option in the ADR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Future architects assume the current implementation is the optimal one, not an explicit trade-off. Without the documented sacrifice, the next person adds more complexity on top instead of understanding why the simpler approach was chosen.

failure-mode.description: Letting one agent both generate options and write the stakeholder summary — without a separate review pass for completeness.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When stakeholder-rendering, agents drop nuance for executives. The agent that generated the technical analysis is not a reliable editor of its own output. A separate review prompt ("does this summary preserve the key risk?") catches losses.
